name: Houston Art Car Parade
description: Annual parade in Houston, Texas featuring hundreds of outlandishly decorated vehicles. One of the largest art car events in the world.

name: Ogoh-Ogoh Parade (Nyepi Eve Parade)
description: The night before the Balinese New Year (Nyepi), villagers parade giant papier-mâché monster effigies (ogoh-ogoh) through the streets before burning them to banish evil spirits.

name: Carnaval de Oruro
description: One of Bolivia's most important cultural events, featuring the diablada (dance of the devils) and elaborate costumes. UNESCO-listed cultural heritage.

name: Coney Island Mermaid Parade
description: Annual arts parade held in Coney Island, Brooklyn. Participants dress as mermaids, sea creatures, and related figures. One of the USA's largest art parades.

name: Barandales Statue
description: Iconic figure in the Easter Holy Week processions in Zamora, Spain.

name: Da-Bach-na-Fahrt (Journey Down the Stream)
description: Whimsical wash tubs row down the stream as part of the Carnival celebration in Schramberg, Germany.

name: Jose Gasparilla II (Tampa Pirate Parade)
description: The country's largest boat parade in Tampa honors the mythical Spanish pirate Jose Gaspar. Features tall ship and pirate invasion.

name: Ivrea Carnival Battle of the Oranges
description: Annual carnival in Ivrea featuring a historical battle with oranges between teams on foot and in horse-drawn carts.

name: Krampusnacht
description: Night of the Krampus - the demonic companion of St. Nicholas. Men in Krampus costumes parade through streets of Austrian and Bavarian towns.

name: Día de los Muertos Procession
description: Day of the Dead procession in Oaxaca, Mexico, featuring elaborate skull costumes, marigolds, and processions to cemeteries.

name: Mardi Gras New Orleans
description: World-famous carnival celebration in New Orleans featuring elaborate float parades, masquerade balls, and beads.

name: Notting Hill Carnival
description: Europe's largest street festival held in Notting Hill, London, celebrating Caribbean culture with colorful costumes and soca music.

name: Buñol Holi – La Tomatina
description: Annual tomato-throwing festival in Buñol, Spain. Participants pelt each other with overripe tomatoes in the streets.

name: Up Helly Aa
description: Annual fire festival in the Shetland Islands, Scotland. Hundreds of participants in Viking costumes march through the streets and burn a full-size galley.

name: Palio di Siena
description: Historic horse race held twice yearly in Siena's Piazza del Campo, preceded by elaborate medieval pageant procession.

name: Hogmanay Street Party
description: Scotland's world-famous New Year celebration featuring torchlight processions, fireworks, and street parties in Edinburgh.

name: Songkran Festival
description: Thai New Year celebrated with water fights in the streets, Buddha image processions, and sand stupas.

name: Rio Carnival
description: The world's largest carnival, famous for its samba school parades through the Sambodrome (Marquês de Sapucaí).

name: Whitby Goth Weekend
description: Twice-yearly gathering of the goth subculture in Whitby, famous as the setting for Bram Stoker's Dracula. Features processions and elaborate costumes.

name: Burning Man
description: Annual counter-cultural festival in Nevada desert. Features giant puppet art installations and effigy burning.
